mandatory_edge_cases: `raw_requested_scope[]` is preserved for audit while `runtime_scope[]` drives downstream semantics.
`masking_context` remains read-side only and must not affect compute, filing, or transport.
Same-manifest retry against a terminal manifest returns the existing `DecisionBundle`.
A pre-start system fault after `SEALED` but before `RunStarted` still finalizes `BLOCKED`.
Replay may reuse frozen post-seal basis instead of inventing a fresh authority read.
`AMENDMENT_GATE` is forbidden during drift preparation and occurs later in the run.
Authority-owned truth and out-of-band corrections stay distinct from internal progression state.

branch_rows.false_path: Continue into runtime-scope resolution and manifest-strategy selection.
branch_rows.trigger_condition: Authorization or access-scope binding fails before any manifest allocation.

entry_conditions: RUN_ENGINE receives `raw_requested_scope[]`, principal context, and any authority-binding context.
`masking_context` may be present for read-side rendering, but execution semantics remain unmasked.
exit_conditions: `runtime_scope[]` is bound from authorization and becomes the only downstream scope vocabulary.
Access-blocked requests may return a low-noise access response before any manifest allocation.
failure_exit_paths: `ACCESS_BLOCKED_RESPONSE(...)` returns a bounded low-noise posture envelope without allocating a manifest.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.
The shell defaults to `LOW_NOISE` and keeps the access result separate from later manifest lifecycle state.

branch_rows.false_path: Proceed to manifest reuse / continuation / replay selection.
branch_rows.trigger_condition: Runtime scope grammar or prior-manifest context fails validation.
branch_rows.true_path: Return `ERROR(...)` before manifest allocation or reuse.

branch_rows.false_path: Continue into fresh, continuation, or replay manifest work.
branch_rows.trigger_condition: The run matches a terminal manifest lineage whose `DecisionBundle` may be safely reused.
branch_rows.true_path: Return the existing `DecisionBundle` instead of allocating a continuation child.

entry_conditions: Authorization resolved a usable `runtime_scope[]` and access binding.
Any prior manifest lineage must be loaded before config inheritance or reuse decisions are chosen.
exit_conditions: Scope grammar, reporting intent, prior-manifest context, and config inheritance mode are resolved.
A same-manifest terminal retry may short-circuit to an existing `DecisionBundle`.
failure_exit_paths: `ERROR(...)` exits on invalid scope grammar or irrecoverable prior-manifest context.
`ExistingDecisionBundleReturned` may short-circuit same-manifest retries against terminal lineage.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.
live_experience.phase_name: Resolve scope intent, validate runtime grammar, load any prior manifest, and choose config inheritance

notes: Config inheritance is frozen as a manifest-strategy choice, not a renderer-side preference.
phases.phase_label: 2. Resolve scope intent, validate runtime grammar, load any prior manifest, and choose config inheritance
phases.phase_name: Resolve scope intent, validate runtime grammar, load any prior manifest, and choose config inheritance

branch_rows.terminal_outcome: Nonterminal lineage fork that governs the rest of the run.
branch_rows.trigger_condition: Reuse strategy chooses fresh manifest, same-manifest reuse, continuation child, or replay child.
branch_rows.true_path: Lineage refs, inheritance modes, and branch-decision audit context are frozen into manifest context.

entry_conditions: The run knows whether it is a fresh manifest, same-manifest reuse, continuation child, or replay child.
Config inheritance and input inheritance choices are available.
exit_conditions: A manifest context is allocated, reused, or continued with durable lineage refs.
The live-experience stream is initialized with `shell_route_key = manifest_id` and monotonic sequencing.
failure_exit_paths: Lineage validation failures stop the run before pre-seal source work starts.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.
live_experience.phase_name: Allocate, continue, or reuse manifest context and initialize the live experience stream

notes: This is the phase that binds raw request lineage to the stable manifest route and shell continuity contract.
phases.phase_label: 3. Allocate, continue, or reuse manifest context and initialize the live experience stream
phases.phase_name: Allocate, continue, or reuse manifest context and initialize the live experience stream

branch_rows.false_path: Continue with the sealed pre-start basis.
branch_rows.terminal_outcome: Usually nonterminal, but may force review or child lineage before seal.
branch_rows.trigger_condition: Late data appears while building or reusing the pre-seal context.
branch_rows.true_path: Spawn child, surface review posture, or exclude late data according to policy bindings.

branch_rows.false_path: Build or recover a fresh pre-seal basis under ordinary collection rules.
branch_rows.terminal_outcome: Nonterminal lineage reuse that constrains later compute and audit expectations.
branch_rows.trigger_condition: The request is a replay-capable lineage path with frozen post-seal basis available.
branch_rows.true_path: Reuse the frozen post-seal basis and replay from deterministic lineage-bound artifacts.

entry_conditions: A manifest exists but has not yet sealed a pre-start execution context.
The run knows whether it may reuse a sealed pre-start context or must rebuild one from source collection.
exit_conditions: Either a reusable sealed pre-start basis is loaded, or a fresh source/canonicalization pipeline has built the frozen input set.
Late-data posture, collection boundary, artifact set, and execution basis hash inputs are known.
failure_exit_paths: Irrecoverable source-collection or replay-precondition failures stop before manifest sealing.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.
live_experience.phase_name: Load or build the sealed pre-start context using a barriered, partition-aware execution plan

notes: `masking_context` is expressly barred from source planning, canonicalization, filing, and transport semantics.
phases.phase_label: 4. Load or build the sealed pre-start context using a barriered, partition-aware execution plan
phases.phase_name: Load or build the sealed pre-start context using a barriered, partition-aware execution plan

branch_rows.false_path: Seal the manifest and permit later `RunStarted` claim.
branch_rows.terminal_outcome: Blocked or review-required pre-start outcome vs nonterminal sealed success.
branch_rows.trigger_condition: The ordered pre-seal gates resolve as block/review or allow seal.
branch_rows.true_path: Persist pre-start terminal context or review-required posture and stop before `RunStarted`.

branch_rows.false_path: Post-start faults use started-run failure handling and may enter `FAILED` semantics.
branch_rows.terminal_outcome: Boundary between blocked pre-start and failed post-start posture.
branch_rows.trigger_condition: A system fault happens before `RunStarted` or after command-side start.
branch_rows.true_path: Pre-start faults finalize `BLOCKED` even if the manifest already sealed.

entry_conditions: Pre-seal artifacts, input freeze, and schema bundle are available or explicitly reused.
The manifest is not yet started, so failures remain pre-start outcomes.
exit_conditions: Ordered pre-seal gates are persisted and either bind a blocked/review posture or allow sealing.
When a new pre-start context was built, the manifest is sealed exactly once.
failure_exit_paths: `FINALIZE_TERMINAL_OUTCOME(...)` persists blocked or review-required pre-start outcomes.
`FINALIZE_RUN_FAILURE(...)` captures irrecoverable pre-start failure without starting the run.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.
live_experience.phase_name: Evaluate the ordered pre-seal gate chain, persist pre-start blocked context if needed, and seal only when a new pre-start context was built

notes: The seal step is atomic with the pre-start persistence boundary, which makes later replay and reuse defensible.
phases.phase_label: 5. Evaluate the ordered pre-seal gate chain, persist pre-start blocked context if needed, and seal only when a new pre-start context was built
phases.phase_name: Evaluate the ordered pre-seal gate chain, persist pre-start blocked context if needed, and seal only when a new pre-start context was built

transaction_spans.label: Persist pre-start context and seal when eligible

entry_conditions: A sealed manifest exists and has not yet been legally started by another writer.
The post-seal execution plan can be published only if a manifest-start claim succeeds.
exit_conditions: A compare-and-swap manifest-start lease is claimed and the first post-seal DAG is atomically published.
`RunStarted` occurs only after a sealed manifest exists.
failure_exit_paths: Start-claim rejection or invalid manifest posture stops progression before worker execution begins.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.
live_experience.phase_name: Claim the sealed manifest and publish the post-seal command DAG atomically

notes: This is the strongest visible proof that the sealed manifest precedes `RunStarted` and worker fan-out.

phases.phase_name: Claim the sealed manifest and publish the post-seal command DAG atomically

transaction_spans.label: Claim manifest start and publish DAG

entry_conditions: The post-seal DAG is durable and a single writer owns the manifest-start lease.
Workers must persist first-class artifacts before any completion signal becomes visible.
exit_conditions: Mandatory post-seal artifacts are adopted into manifest outputs.
Projection lag may continue, but command-side truth has the required compute, risk, graph, and related outputs.
failure_exit_paths: Missing mandatory stage artifacts or invalid adopted outputs escalate as run failure after start.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.
live_experience.phase_name: Await post-seal command DAG completion and adopt mandatory outputs

notes: The DAG boundary separates command truth from slower read-model materialization without losing lineage determinism.
phases.phase_label: 7. Await post-seal command DAG completion and adopt mandatory outputs
phases.phase_name: Await post-seal command DAG completion and adopt mandatory outputs

entry_conditions: Core compute, risk, graph, and retained evidence posture are available after post-seal adoption.
exit_conditions: The retention evidence gate is recorded and becomes part of the ordered non-access gate chain.
failure_exit_paths: This phase records binding posture but does not itself finalize the run; terminalization happens in later decisive branches.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.
Retention limitations are legal constraints on what can be shown or relied upon, not client-side decoration.

branch_rows.false_path: Proceed into drift, trust, amendment, and filing packet work.
branch_rows.trigger_condition: Authority or filing-readiness validation yields non-pass posture before packet preparation.
branch_rows.true_path: Emit filing or amendment gate posture and finalize blocked/review-required outcome early.

entry_conditions: Non-access pre-trust gate context is available and the run can consult authority-facing comparison basis.
Authority state must remain distinct from internal progression state and packet intent.
exit_conditions: Authority comparison basis, parity result, and parity gate posture are persisted.
Early filing-readiness or amendment-intent terminalization may already have returned.
failure_exit_paths: `FINALIZE_TERMINAL_OUTCOME(...)` may return early for filing-readiness or amendment-intent validation failure.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.
live_experience.phase_name: Authority context, comparison basis, parity, and parity gate

notes: Authority calculations use controlled outbox + inbox recovery, so inline waits never invent unnormalized external truth.

phases.phase_name: Authority context, comparison basis, parity, and parity gate

entry_conditions: Authority comparison and parity posture are known.
The run can select the lawful drift baseline and evaluate retroactive impact.
exit_conditions: Drift, amendment-window context, retroactive impact, and amendment case posture are prepared for later gates.
failure_exit_paths: No direct terminalization occurs here; this phase prepares downstream drift/amendment consequences only.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.

phases.phase_name: Drift posture and amendment posture preparation

entry_conditions: Parity, retention, drift, and override context are available.
exit_conditions: Trust summary, trust gate, and command-side filing case posture are persisted.
failure_exit_paths: This phase does not directly return, but its gate posture may trigger trust-based terminalization in the next read-model phase.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.

phases.phase_label: 11. Trust synthesis and command-side case state
phases.phase_name: Trust synthesis and command-side case state

entry_conditions: Trust posture and the ordered gate chain are stable enough to emit operator workflow consequences.
exit_conditions: Workflow items and immediate-consequence posture are refreshed for the current manifest.
failure_exit_paths: No direct terminal return occurs here.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.

notes: Workflow remains downstream of gate posture; it does not replace or reinterpret decisive legal state.

phases.phase_name: Workflow planning and immediate consequence refresh

branch_rows.false_path: Continue into amendment and filing stages.
branch_rows.terminal_outcome: Completed, blocked, or review-required terminalization before filing logic.
branch_rows.trigger_condition: Trust posture is already decisive and no later amendment or filing stage remains required.
branch_rows.true_path: Publish live projections and return a terminal bundle from trust posture.

entry_conditions: The run has stable trust posture and can publish live read-model projections for the shell.
Projection workers must remain read-side only and may finish after `DecisionBundle` persistence.
exit_conditions: Composite low-noise shell surfaces are refreshed and the run either proceeds onward or terminalizes on trust posture.
failure_exit_paths: `FINALIZE_TERMINAL_OUTCOME(...)` may return on decisive trust posture before amendment or filing stages.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.
live_experience.phase_name: Publish live read-model projections and conditionally terminalize on trust posture

notes: This phase is the clearest command-truth versus read-side boundary: `ExperienceDelta` is operational, not the legal record.
phases.phase_label: 13. Publish live read-model projections and conditionally terminalize on trust posture
phases.phase_name: Publish live read-model projections and conditionally terminalize on trust posture

branch_rows.false_path: No amendment progression occurs and filing logic remains ordinary.
branch_rows.terminal_outcome: May terminalize blocked/review-required on amendment gate or continue to filing.
branch_rows.trigger_condition: Runtime scope requests amendment intent, amendment submit, or neither.
branch_rows.true_path: Intent-to-amend readiness and later submit readiness remain distinct state-machine progressions.

entry_conditions: Amendment-related scope was requested or amendment posture otherwise needs decisive evaluation.
Drift and amendment-preparation artifacts are already persisted from phase 10.
exit_conditions: `AMENDMENT_GATE` and any intent-to-amend readiness posture are persisted.
The run either continues with a ready amendment path or terminalizes with blocked/review posture.
failure_exit_paths: `FINALIZE_TERMINAL_OUTCOME(...)` returns when amendment gate posture is blocking or review-required.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.
This is the first lawful place the engine may evaluate `AMENDMENT_GATE`; drift preparation alone is not enough.

entry_conditions: Filing-related scope is requested or amendment submit requires packet preparation.
Trust, parity, authority context, and amendment posture are available.
exit_conditions: Filing-readiness context and `FILING_GATE` posture are persisted.
A filing packet may be prepared and hardened to `APPROVED_TO_SUBMIT` when lawful.
failure_exit_paths: `FINALIZE_TERMINAL_OUTCOME(...)` returns for blocked or review-required filing posture.
`FINALIZE_RUN_FAILURE(...)` guards packet-manifest binding mismatch.
`ERROR(AMENDMENT_CASE_NOT_READY_TO_SUBMIT)` prevents invalid amendment submission.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.
live_experience.phase_name: Authority calculation, filing readiness, packet preparation, and filing gate

notes: Internal filing intent and packet hardening remain distinct from authority-owned acceptance or confirmation.
phases.phase_label: 15. Authority calculation, filing readiness, packet preparation, and filing gate
phases.phase_name: Authority calculation, filing readiness, packet preparation, and filing gate

branch_rows.false_path: End without transmit while retaining packet or trust posture as the latest truth.
branch_rows.terminal_outcome: May remain pending external confirmation, complete, or block/review depending on authority outcome.
branch_rows.trigger_condition: Runtime scope requests actual submission and the packet is approved to submit.
branch_rows.true_path: Execute governed transmit, bounded recovery, and authority reconciliation.

entry_conditions: A filing packet is approved for submit and runtime scope actually requests submission.
Submission must still pass send-time dedupe, binding, and gate checks.
exit_conditions: Submission gate posture, submission record state, authority request lineage, and any reconciliation outputs are persisted.
The live shell now distinguishes transmit-pending, pending-ack, confirmed, rejected, unknown, and out-of-band authority posture.
failure_exit_paths: `FINALIZE_TERMINAL_OUTCOME(...)` returns when `SUBMISSION_GATE` blocks or requires review before transmit.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.
live_experience.phase_name: Submission enqueue, governed transmit, inbox-normalized recovery, and bounded reconciliation

notes: Authority-owned truth and out-of-band corrections remain separate from the engine's internal packet and submission intent states.
phases.phase_label: 16. Submission enqueue, governed transmit, inbox-normalized recovery, and bounded reconciliation
phases.phase_name: Submission enqueue, governed transmit, inbox-normalized recovery, and bounded reconciliation

transaction_spans.label: Queue transmit intent and advance packet/submission state

entry_conditions: A submission exists and reconciliation has produced some authority-side truth or ambiguity.
exit_conditions: Post-authority drift, retroactive impact, amendment window, and amendment case refresh are persisted.
Workflow escalation opens when authority-confirmed state is internally superseded or further drift action is needed.
failure_exit_paths: No direct return occurs here; this phase prepares any post-authority follow-up that survives terminalization.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.
This phase preserves the difference between accepted authority truth and the engine's later observation of new contradictions or amendment need.

entry_conditions: Command-side work is done, or the run is waiting on bounded external confirmation.
The engine must normalize terminal workflow refs and shell checkpoint vocabulary before returning.
exit_conditions: A terminal `DecisionBundle` is persisted with final reasons, retained refs, and normalized workflow posture.
The live shell is refreshed into terminal posture without losing authority-pending or out-of-band distinctions.
failure_exit_paths: The phase itself is the final terminalization and return path for successful, blocked, or externally pending outcomes.

live_experience.command_vs_read_side_boundary: Every `ExperienceDelta` remains read-side only; command truth and state transitions must already be durable.
notes: Low-noise shells keep one mounted route keyed by `manifest_id`.
Reduced-motion clients must preserve semantics without relying on motion tokens.
The terminal live-experience frame is operational read-side state only; it is not the legal record of what happened.
